title: IRDAI Links Insurance KMP Pay to Customer Outcomes, Mandates Public Disclosures
author: BimaNiti
summary: IRDAI amended corporate governance norms to link KMP remuneration to customer-centric metrics including claim settlement speed, grievance redressal, and product performance.
content:
IRDAI on Tuesday amended its Corporate Governance for Insurers Regulations, 2024, introducing a framework tying executive compensation to customer outcomes.

Key Changes: 50% of KMP variable pay linked to six mandatory parameters including claim responsiveness, grievance redressal, Ind AS implementation (10%), and removal of dark patterns (10%). Public disclosure of financial data quarterly, and claims/grievances/product data monthly.

Sources: Hindu BusinessLine (May 26, 2026), Economic Times (May 26, 2026)

title: LIC Q4 FY26: Net Profit Surges 23% to Rs 23,467 Crore, Board Declares Rs 10 Dividend and 1:1 Bonus
author: BimaNiti
summary: LIC reported a 23% YoY rise in Q4 net profit to Rs 23,467 crore, with FY26 PAT reaching a record Rs 57,419 crore. The board declared a Rs 10/share dividend and a 1:1 bonus issue.
content:
Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) reported a strong Q4 FY26 performance, with consolidated net profit rising 23% YoY to Rs 23,467 crore. For the full year FY26, profit after tax reached a record Rs 57,419 crore, up 19% from Rs 48,151 crore in FY25.

Key Highlights: Q4 APE rose 22% to Rs 22,954 crore; VNB surged 66.7% to Rs 5,891 crore. FY26 VNB grew 42% to Rs 14,179 crore; VNB margin improved 360 bps to 21.2%. Solvency ratio improved to 2.35 from 2.11. AUM stood at Rs 57.3 lakh crore.

Sources: Economic Times (May 21, 2026), Business Standard (May 21, 2026), CNBC TV18 (May 21, 2026)

title: New India Assurance Q4: Profit Jumps 61% to Rs 558 Crore on Investment Income
author: BimaNiti
summary: New India Assurance reported 61% YoY growth in Q4 PAT to Rs 558 crore. FY26 PAT rose 40% to Rs 1,384 crore.
content:
New India Assurance (NIA) reported a 61% YoY jump in Q4 net profit to Rs 558 crore. For FY26, net profit climbed 40% to Rs 1,384 crore, while gross written premium grew 8.2% to Rs 47,174 crore.

Key Highlights: Market share increased to 12.74%. Combined ratio at 122.57% (FY26) due to wage revision costs of Rs 3,525 crore. Adjusted combined ratio at 116.67%. Investment income grew 38% to Rs 11,112 crore. Solvency ratio at 1.84.

Sources: Asia Insurance Post (May 11, 2026), AlphaStreet, TradeBrains (May 12, 2026)

title: Standalone Health Insurers Lead with 17.89% Premium Growth as Health Awareness Rises
author: BimaNiti
summary: Standalone health insurers recorded 17.89% growth with premium collections of Rs 35,815 crore up to January 2026.
content:
The health insurance segment continues to be the standout performer. Standalone health insurers posted 17.89% growth with collections of Rs 35,815 crore up to January 2026.

Source: IRDAI 134th Meeting Report, Business Standard (April 2026)

title: India's Insurance Penetration Stable at 3.7% as Density Improves Marginally to USD 97
author: BimaNiti
summary: India's insurance penetration remained at 3.7% in FY2024-25 while insurance density increased from USD 95 to USD 97.
content:
IRDAI Annual Report 2024-25: Overall insurance penetration at 3.7% (life: 2.7%, non-life: 1.0%). Insurance density increased modestly from USD 95 to USD 97. Total premium of Rs 11.93 lakh crore. Claims paid of Rs 8.36 lakh crore. AUM at Rs 74.44 lakh crore.

Source: IRDAI Annual Report 2024-25

title: 100% FDI in Insurance: Global Insurers Line Up to Enter Indian Market
author: BimaNiti
summary: With the SBSR Act enabling 100% FDI from February 2026, several global insurers are exploring increased stakes in their Indian subsidiaries.
content:
The landmark increase in FDI limit to 100% under the SBSR Act is attracting global interest. India's position as the 10th largest insurance market globally with 1.8% market share makes it an attractive destination for global insurance capital.

Source: PIB India, Industry Analysis

title: SBI General Insurance Crosses Rs 15,000 Crore GDP Milestone in FY26
author: BimaNiti
summary: SBI General Insurance crossed Rs 15,000 crore in gross direct premium for the first time with 14.5% growth, outpacing the industry by 1.6 times.
content:
SBI General Insurance reported a strong FY26 performance, with Gross Direct Premium (GDP) rising 14.5% YoY to Rs 15,904 crore — crossing the Rs 15,000 crore milestone. Profit after tax rose 8.7% to Rs 553 crore.

Key Highlights: Private and SAHI market share up 27 bps to 7.17%. Health grew 27%, Motor 16%, Personal Accident 40%. Loss ratio improved to 78.3% from 82.4%. Solvency ratio at 1.90x.

Sources: Economic Times (April 24, 2026), Business Standard (April 24, 2026)

title: SBI Life FY26: NBP Grows 20% to Rs 42,550 Crore, VNB Margin Holds at 27.5%
author: BimaNiti
summary: SBI Life reported 20% NBP growth to Rs 42,550 crore with VNB margins at the upper end of guidance. GWP crossed Rs 1 lakh crore for the first time.
content:
SBI Life Insurance delivered a steady FY26 performance, with New Business Premium (NBP) growing 20% YoY to Rs 42,550 crore. Gross Written Premium (GWP) crossed the Rs 1 lakh crore milestone at Rs 1,01,290 crore, up 19%.

Key Highlights: APE grew 13% to Rs 24,270 crore; VNB grew 12% to Rs 6,670 crore. VNB margin at 27.5% — upper end of guidance. IEV grew 15% to Rs 80,790 crore. 13th month persistency improved 53 bps to 87.9%.

Sources: SBI Life Press Release (April 22, 2026), ICICI Direct Research (April 23, 2026)

title: Mid-Tier Life Insurers Gain Ground: Retail Premium Growth Outpaces Top 5 Players
author: BimaNiti
summary: IRDAI's FY26 premium data reveals mid-tier insurers growing at 15.7% in retail premium, delivering 83% of the top-tier's retail gain.
content:
IRDAI's FY2025-26 first year premium data reveals a structural shift. Mid-tier insurers added Rs 4,297 crore in retail regular premium in FY26. 52.7% of mid-tier's total premium comes from retail business vs 22.2% for Top 5. Emerging insurers grew 52.4%.

Source: NDTV Profit, April 27, 2026

title: ICICI Lombard Q4: GDPI Growth Surges 18.2%, Health Insurance Leads at 38.2%
author: BimaNiti
summary: ICICI Lombard reported 18.2% GDPI growth in Q4 FY26, nearly double the industry rate. Retail health insurance premiums surged 55.6%.
content:
ICICI Lombard General Insurance reported a strong Q4 FY26, with Gross Direct Premium Income (GDPI) growing 18.2% to Rs 7,340 crore — well above the industry growth of 10.9%. Net profit rose 7.3% YoY to Rs 546.6 crore.

Key Highlights: Health GDPI grew 38.2% in Q4; retail health individual premiums surged 54% for FY26. Combined ratio improved 130 bps to 101.2%. Solvency ratio stable at 2.67x.

Sources: Reuters (April 15, 2026), Business Standard (April 15-16, 2026), ICICI Lombard Earnings Call (April 15, 2026)

title: GST Exemption on Health Insurance Drives Premium Surge in H2FY26
author: BimaNiti
summary: The GST exemption on retail health insurance policies from September 2025 significantly boosted health insurance premiums in H2FY26.
content:
The government's decision to reduce GST rates on retail health insurance policies to nil in September 2025 has proven to be a significant catalyst. Standalone health insurers posted 19.4% YoY growth in FY26.

Sources: Business Standard, ICRA Insurance Sector Report (April 2026)

title: CareEdge: Non-Life Insurance Sector Grew 8.6% in FY26, Health Insurance Leads Growth
author: BimaNiti
summary: CareEdge reported that the non-life insurance sector grew 8.6% in FY26, with health insurance emerging as the primary growth driver.
content:
CareEdge Ratings released its analysis of the non-life insurance sector performance for FY26, reporting industry GDPI growth of 8.6% to approximately Rs 3.3 lakh crore. The health insurance segment was the primary growth driver, supported by the GST exemption.

Key Findings: Standalone Health Insurers outperformed with ~19% growth. Excluding the crop segment, the industry GDPI grew 13.4% in 11M FY26. ICRA expects GDPI to grow 9.7-10.6% in FY2027.

Source: CareEdge Ratings, ICRA Insurance Sector Report (April 2026)

title: Non-Life Insurers Log 9.3% Premium Growth in FY26 Amid GST Relief
author: BimaNiti
summary: The non-life insurance industry reported 9.3% YoY growth to Rs 3.36 trillion in FY26, aided by the health insurance segment after GST rate rationalisation.
content:
The non-life insurance industry reported 9.3% year-on-year growth in premiums to Rs 3.36 trillion in FY2025-26. General insurance companies: 8% YoY growth to Rs 2.79 trillion. Standalone health insurers: 19.4% YoY growth to Rs 45,865.81 crore.

Source: Business Standard, April 9, 2026

title: IRDAI Notifies Ind AS Implementation Framework for Insurers, Effective April 1, 2026
author: BimaNiti
summary: IRDAI issued the final framework for Ind AS 117 implementation, mandating parallel reporting for two years and a one-year forbearance option.
content:
IRDAI issued a comprehensive circular on April 1, 2026, providing the detailed framework for implementation of Indian Accounting Standards (Ind AS) by insurers, effective from April 1, 2026.

Key Provisions: All insurers must prepare financial statements under Ind AS. Parallel reporting (Ind AS + IGAAP) required for the first two years. Independent validation from IRDAI-empanelled auditors in the first year. One-year forbearance option available with board-approved action plan.

Sources: IRDAI Circular (April 1, 2026), IRDAI Exposure Draft

title: Ind AS 117 Comes Into Effect: New Accounting Standard Transforms Insurance Financial Reporting
author: BimaNiti
summary: Ind AS 117 came into effect from April 2026, fundamentally changing how insurers recognise revenue, report profits, and communicate financial performance.
content:
Ind AS 117 became effective from April 2026, representing one of the most significant changes to insurance financial reporting. Insurance profits will emerge over the period of service, fundamentally changing earnings visibility. The standard pushes the industry from premium growth optics to earnings quality and risk-adjusted returns.

Source: FE BFSI Analysis (May 2026), ICAI/IRDAI Guidelines

title: IRDAI Approves Allianz Jio Reinsurance and Kiwi General Insurance at 134th Meeting
author: BimaNiti
summary: IRDAI granted certificates of registration to Allianz Jio Reinsurance (50:50 JV between Jio Financial and Allianz) and Kiwi General Insurance.
content:
IRDAI approved two new insurance entities at its 134th meeting: Allianz Jio Reinsurance Limited (50:50 JV between Jio Financial Services and Allianz Group, capital base Rs 300 crore) and Kiwi General Insurance Limited (backed by WestBridge Capital, founded by Neelesh Garg).

Tapan Singhel, Chairman of the General Insurance Council, commented: 'India needs more than a thousand insurers to address the protection needs of every citizen.'

Sources: The Hindu (March 9, 2026), Business Standard (March 9, 2026)

title: IRDAI Reviews Insurance Sector Performance; Total Premium Collections Reach Rs 10.51 Lakh Crore
author: BimaNiti
summary: At its 134th meeting, IRDAI reviewed industry performance noting total premium collections of Rs 10.51 lakh crore with 11.45% growth.
content:
IRDAI held its 134th meeting on March 9, 2026. Industry-level premium for FY2025-26 up to January 2026 reached Rs 10.51 lakh crore with growth rate of 11.45%. Life insurers: Rs 7.67 lakh crore with 12.27% growth. General Insurers: Rs 2.83 lakh crore with 9.3% growth.

Source: IRDAI Official Release, March 2026

title: RBI Proposes Stricter Bancassurance Norms: Two-Tier Model for Banks Selling Insurance
author: BimaNiti
summary: RBI issued draft directions for bancassurance, creating a clear separation between agency (fee-based distribution) and referral (one-time referral fee) models.
content:
RBI on February 11, 2026, proposed a comprehensive overhaul of rules governing how banks sell and refer third-party financial products, including insurance. The new framework takes effect from April 1, 2026.

Two Distinct Models: Agency model (fee-based distribution, no risk participation) and Referral model (one-time referral fee only, no commissions or trails). Third-party products limited to regulated products only.

Sources: RBI Press Release (February 11, 2026), ET BFSI (February 11, 2026)

title: Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha Act Comes Into Effect: 100% FDI in Insurance Now Operational
author: BimaNiti
summary: The Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha Act came into force, raising the FDI limit to 100% and introducing enhanced policyholder protection measures.
content:
The Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha (Amendment of Insurance Laws) Act, 2025 came into force with effect from February 5, 2026. FDI limit raised from 74% to 100%. Maximum penalty increased from Rs 1 crore to Rs 10 crore. IRDAI empowered to order disgorgement of wrongful gains.

Source: PIB India, IRDAI Official Release (March 2026)

title: Swiss Re Forecasts India as Fastest-Growing Major Insurance Market at 6.9% CAGR Over 2026-2030
author: BimaNiti
summary: Swiss Re projects Indian insurance premium growth to accelerate to 6.9% over 2026-2030, outpacing China, the US and Western European markets.
content:
Swiss Re published a comprehensive analysis projecting that India's insurance market will grow at an annual rate of 6.9% over 2026 to 2030 in real terms — higher than major emerging and advanced insurance markets. Health insurance expected to grow by 7.2% per year, motor insurance by 7.5% per year.

Source: Swiss Re Press Release, January 19, 2026
